page: Vultr vcg-b200-248c-2826g-1536vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server featuring an x86_64 architecture with 248 Intel physical cores and a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 2826.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 11.4 GB of RAM per core.

The server integrates eight NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs, delivering a total of 1536 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the configuration includes 13312 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

This hardware profile represents a high-density resource configuration designed for massive parallel processing. The qualitative cost efficiency is defined by the consolidation of eight Blackwell GPUs and over 2.8 TB of system memory within a single bare metal instance, reducing the need for multi-node clustering for large workloads. Appropriate workloads include large language model training, high-performance computing, large-scale data science jobs, and intensive GPU-accelerated database operations.
description: Vultr vcg-b200-248c-2826g-1536vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 248 Intel physical cores with a shared CPU allocation and 2826.0 GB of system memory, yielding 11.4 GB of RAM per core. The primary hardware accelerator setup consists of eight NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs providing a total of 1536 GB of VRAM. Local storage is supported by 13312 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. This server is engineered for high-density parallel computing, offering significant resource consolidation that minimizes multi-node overhead. It is designed for workloads such as large-scale artificial intelligence model training, deep learning, complex data science simulations, and GPU-accelerated database tasks.

tagline: A high-density bare metal server featuring eight NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs, 2826 GB RAM, and 13.3 TB NVMe SSD storage.

Eight NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs with 1536 GB of total VRAM.
